items.title: Hundreds gather in Alice Springs ahead of Pine Gap protest

children.content: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

gallery.altText: Two older women smile for the camera.
A man walks though smoke in front of two women with their backs to the camera.
An older woman wearing a pink beanie.

thumbnail.altText: Two older women smile for the camera.

items.title: Outback tour company sues vehicle manufacturer over fatal NT rollover

children.content: An Alice Springs tour company is suing a vehicle manufacturer for negligence and breach of contract following a bus crash that killed a 69-year-old man in 2022.

image.altText: Patrick Bedford from Tourism Central Australia says JobKeeper needs to be extended

items.title: NT Police officer fires gun twice while 'trying to stop stolen vehicle'

children.content: A Northern Territory police officer has fired two shots at a vehicle while responding to a reported armed break-in in Palmerston.

image.altText: Close up of Queensland Police Service-issued black gun in holster on navy pants

items.title: Aircraft from 15 countries touch down in Darwin ahead of war games

children.content: Military aircraft and aviators from 20 countries land in Darwin ahead of Exercise Pitch Black 2026, with the biennial war games poised to begin.

items.title: Early-onset bowel cancer becoming more prevalent as signs are missed

children.content: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

image.altText: A young woman in a white shirt sitting in her living room.

items.title: 'Bring back public flogging': Inquiry hears of vicious online trolling

children.content: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

image.altText: Racism Hearing Inquiry committee

items.title: Fugitive busted pretending to fish as driver stuck in outback 'nightmare'

children.content: After agreeing to help smuggle an alleged drug trafficker out of Australia, Stephen Roche found himself at the centre of a living "nightmare", stranded in the outback without funds or friends, court documents have revealed.

items.title: Argentina fans stage Times Square takeover ahead of World Cup final

children.content: Times Square has been the epicentre for World Cup celebrations throughout the tournament, but it reached new heights as Argentina fans swamped the "centre of the universe" ahead of the final against Spain in nearby New Jersey.

items.title: The Houdini of Boggo Road escaped the prison in 1946. He shouldn't have taken a friend

items.title: In a secret safe at this luxury house, police struck gold

items.title: From 40 cartographers to two, street directories are nearing end of the road
